entry.title: Intelligent ERP use cases in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Hello SAP Cloud ERP Community 🔊

the second major release of the year is out since July 24. You certainly have read the very comprehensive release blog from Bert Schulze.

In this blog, as part of our intelligent ERP blog post series, I'll share the key innovations with as always the focus on "intelligent technologies" like Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management framework, and their impact on business processes. We will look at some use cases for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2408, delivered to the customers on July 24.

If you'd like to have a quicker understand of this blog, we have summarised the key "intelligent ERP" innovations in this video for your convenience: 

 

🎧User Experience Update with SAP Joule and My Home

Now let's start with an innovation that is changing completely the way customers will work with SAP: of course I will start with Joule, our natural-language, generative AI copilot, embedded into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. SAP Joule is also embedded in other SAP solutions like SAP SuccessFactors, with the ambition to enrich then entire SAP portfolio. Joule is your co-pilot. Yes you are right, this has a history at SAP, for those who know  to the former SAP Co-Pilot assistant, now brought to the next level thanks to the GenAI revolutionising technology. SAP Joule completely changes the way users works with the system and their key application: it is one click away, in-place, increases efficiency, shares insights and analytics, always in the right business context.

Simply said: SAP Joule will support you in getting your work done faster, making every task simpler, reducing mundane tasks, and driving better business outcomes in a secure and compliant way.

After a success early adopter program that is currently running, we have great news: Joule is planned to be in general availability with the next “continuous delivery” later this year. And make sure to follow the enablement offering of our team (see the wheel below for more details) as we will share the key learnings of the early adopter program with our ecosystem, like at the next Product Expert Training planned in September 2024 for our partners.

In a nutshel: stay tuned for an amazing SAP experience transforming how you will interact with the SAP suite!

Next I'd like to continue with the focus on the end user, and present some additional User Experience innovations. We have already introduced "My Home". With the My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, end users benefit from a personalized interface tailored to their needs, with important to-dos and insights readily accessible.

In this release, we offer new customers intelligent app recommendations. Those are based on several factors such as usage of the app, geography, or access. We also offer intelligent generation of pre-delivered cards.

Additionally, we support situations of the standard situations framework, as well as of the extended situations framework in the To Dos section.

💡SAP Business AI overview

Let's move on to Business AI. What does this term mean at all? Business AI is simply SAP Artificial Intelligence  built for the business, AI built for you, available in all line-of-business solutions. It elevates the value of the ERP process with an approach unique in the industry. These are the learnings of more than 5 decades of partnership and projects with thousands of customers, running their businesses across all lines-of-business, managing a gold mine of data. SAP Business AI spans across the entire SAP portfolio, as depicted in this best-of-suite illustration.

With this release, we have embedded AI at the heart of several business processes such as Finance, Manufacturing and Project Management.

Let’s pick Finance as example, where I’d like to mention 2 innovations in beta shipments: The first one a reporting case, that make it possible to use generative AI to analyze and summarize the data from a cost-center-review booklet, into financial business insights, supported by our digital assistant Joule

The second example is a quite specific configuration case for the Unites States.. It offers to leverage Gen AI in a US Tax Configuration solution leveraging Joule, that massively helps reduce complexity of this task. This will speak to all experts is this domain, what a release...

Next on - resource management, In our Technology Industry – it is all about project management, with the imperative to optimise all kind of resources: money, time, people,.... Is this field Artificial Intelligence can massively improve business processes too, and provide a very different project management experience.

I’d like to introduce an innovation that will help all of us, and help get to a more efficient project management planning and staffing. In the new SAP Project and Resource Management, resource management is supported by a new Intelligent matching of employees, to the expected skills on the project.

Searching skilled employees for a resource request has never been easier thanks to the advanced skill match supports. This is part of beta testing as well. 

 

📡Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management framework update

To close this update, I’ll dig into the Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management framework – short ISLM.

ISLM is a framework that allows you to perform lifecycle management operations on machine learning scenarios.

For this release, let me mention 2 scenarios. The first one is a GenAI use cases support: ISLM acts as the bridge between the application and the LLM models, and allows exploration and activation of out of the box scenarios

The second example is called "Auto enablement (turnkey) of Machine Learning use cases". As the name says, it means less manual effort, not specific knowledge needed, more outcome of the business users, when is comes to search, activation and usage of machine learning cases provided by SAP.

Thanks for your time reading this update. Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Sourcing & Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Hey procurement fellows,

I'm back from parental leave and bring the energy down to this new blog giving you insights on SAP S/4HANA Cloud release 2408 for Sourcing & Procurement. To get an overview on the release highlights, please watch this video by my colleague @sangdao. Kudos go to the colleagues in development!

 


For other releases please see here.

Operational Procurement

  • Shipping notification in document flow of self-service purchase requisition 
  • Document Management System (DMS) attachments on the purchase order header
  • Edit during approval for supplier confirmation
  • Sending DMS-based header attachments in EDI, print and e-mail channels for purchase order output

Contract Management

  • Versioning in purchase contracts

Central Procurement

  • Mass change of centrally managed purchase requisitions in offline mode
  • Display of overall invoice consumption on central contract item and distribution levels

Operational Procurement

Shipping notification in document flow of self-service purchase requisition 

With this release we enable requestors to track the shipment of their goods by pulling up any order acknowledgments or advanced shipping notifications from their respective supplier. This way we improve requestors' visibility of purchase requisition ordering and shipping status by integrating shipping notifications into the self-service requisition document flow. For more information, see: My Purchase Requisitions - New

 

Back to top 

Document Management System (DMS) attachments on the purchase order header

With this feature, purchasers can add DMS (Document Management System) attachments to the purchase order header via the Manage Purchase Orders or Create Purchase Order – Advance app. The attachments can be sent along with the purchase order to the supplier. With this innovation you will be able to change the default customization. We speed up the processing with the Harmonized Document Management (HDM) for purchase order header attachments in the SAP Fiori app Manage Purchase Orders. HDM allows your purchasers to work with documents from different document frameworks in a single user interface once activated by the configuration expert. This way we allow to change the default customization and let your restrict the use of new purchase order attachments through Generic Object Services (GOS) attachments, i.e. no new GOS attachments can be created, existing GOS attachments are visible. Your benefit is an increased efficiency through a similar behavior for header attachments. For more information, see: Manage Purchase Orders and Create Purchase Order - Advanced

 

 Back to top 

Edit during approval for supplier confirmation

Purchasers can now edit supplier confirmations that are presently undergoing approval workflow. When a supplier confirmation that is presently undergoing approval workflow is changed, the approval workflow is restarted. This improves flexibility and useability of supplier confirmation which requires to be updated before getting finally approved. For more information, see: Manage Workflows for Supplier Confirmations

 

 

 

Back to top 

Sending DMS-based header attachments in EDI, print and e-mail channels for purchase order output

As of now DMS attachments which are stored in the purchase order header level can be sent through output generated via EDI, Print and/or Email channel. The DMS attachments (Doc.Type SL1) that are configured for the purchase order outputs can be sent through purchase order output channels, when HDM (Harmonized Document Management) is active. Configuration experts can activate it via SSCUI ID 105346. DMS attachment allows to you classify attachment by document type. Each document type for output can be configured individually. This improves efficiency for  business user in attaching and sending important documents to supplier for the respective purchase order at the header level. For more information, see: Purchase Order Header Attachments in EDI Channel Output

 

 

Back to top 

Contract Management

Versioning in purchase contracts

A purchase contract is an agreement between a buyer and a supplier with agreed pricing, quantity, terms, and conditions. Since purchase contracts are long-term documents, they are subject to change over time. Versioning of purchase contracts enables you to track different versions of the contract each time you make changes to an active purchase contract. When you make changes to the fields that are set up for versioning, a new version is created. Depending on the approval process, this new version then becomes the active document. This way we enable you to retain an active purchase contract as the operational contract until the new version of the purchase contract is approved. In addition, it allows the creation of follow-on purchase orders even if the new version of the purchase contract is still in approval.

Purchasers can compare changes between two versions of a purchase contract along with pricing information, thus helping you track changes for a long-term purchase contract. In order to make this available for end users, configuration experts need to define version-relevant fields in SSCUI ID 103497 and set up version management in SSCUI ID 103498, i.e. activate versioning for specific contract types and purchase organizations. For more details watch the demo video below.

 

Back to top  

SAP Ariba Central Procurement*

Mass change of centrally managed purchase requisitions in offline mode

WIth release 2408 central purchasers are able to create, update and delete purchase requisitions in all the backend systems connected to the central procurement hub by offline Microsoft Excel up-/downloads.For example to close or delete unwanted requisitions, generate demands from external planning systems, or add missing accounting information to an Unknown account assignment. A central purchaser can also trigger a simulation run of planned mass changes and display potential update errors before the actual release of the mass changes. This will increase flexibility and usability in the mass management of purchase requisitions in a multi-ERP procurement landscape through Microsoft Excel uploads. For more information, see: Process Purchase Requisitions Centrally

 

 Back to top 

Display of overall invoice consumption on central contract item and distribution levels

Central purchasers are now able to track the usage and performance of their central contracts. Showing the overall invoice percentage as a consumption bar at item and distribution levels, a central purchaser can monitor and compare the invoice consumption to the goods receipt consumption and overall consumption of a given contract. We hereby improve transparency of actual invoice consumption directly within the central contract without the need to navigate into each connected system. This saves time and money. For more information, see: Manage Central Purchase Contracts

 

 Back to top

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn 😉

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 60-minute-long recording and a presentation for Sourcing & Procurement as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

 

 

 

Back to top

entry.title: Customer Projects with T&E Billing & PoC-based Revenue Recognition
entry.content:
Relevant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.

Co-authored by Stefan Walz, Sven Nachtigall, Frank Hennrich, Volkmar Zahn, and Andreas Hammerschmidt.

 

Introduction

In a Time & Expense (T&E) scenario, billing and realized revenue are based on cost postings such as time and expense confirmations. With every time recording or expense posting, revenue is calculated based on the determined billing material and its sales price. If the maintained sales price is, for example, 100 EUR/h, the realized revenue for 1 hour time recording is also 100 EUR/h. A more detailed explanation on revenue recognition for T&E projects can be found in this blog.

However, not all T&E contracts allow full realization of revenue based on the maintained sales price. Contracts can also include a shared risk agreement, in which the supplier and customer agree on a certain planned effort for delivering a certain scope. If the actual efforts exceed the planned ones, the burden of the additional unplanned efforts are shared between the supplier and customer. As a result, the recognized revenue should not only consider delivered effort with normal sales prices, but should incorporate as well already known future additional efforts which can be charged with a reduced sales price only. While billing is carried out periodically based on efforts incurred to date, revenue accounting considers the progress of the project based on the cost-based percentage of completion.

This blog describes the steps and procedure to set up and manage such a customer project arrangement.

 

Solution architecture and capabilities

Costs and revenues should not be recognized as they occur but should consider future additional efforts at lower rates. underlying foundation for the cost-based Percentage of Completion (PoC) revenue recognition in a T&E scenario is the new financial planning version PRO03. This planning version, similar to the current ongoing planning version PRO02, is populated through the application job "Transfer Project Forecast to Finance". However, there is one significant difference with PRO03 compared to the others: Costs and revenues are distributed over periods; only one sum is assigned to the fiscal year in which the project end date falls.

Additionally, there's a new revenue recognition key, SPTMSR (Cost-Based PoC for DIP based T&E projects), which executes revenue recognition as described above. It is recommended to create a new contract material (for example, P005), from which this new revenue recognition key is derived. The configuration activity ‘Derivation of Recognition Key for Projects’ (SSCUI 102517) can be used to configure SPTMSR for the necessary company codes in combination with contract type PS02 (Time and Expenses) and the new contract material. The latter is then used as the material in the sales order item.

Project plan categories in Finance

The original project planning and agreed standard work for delivering a specific service include 100 hours of Senior Consultant work at a cost rate of 65 EUR/h and a sales price of 100 EUR/h. Although the project has not yet started, the project manager is already second work package that will be needed. As per the contract agreement with the customer, the sales price can only be 50 EUR/h for this package. This is accomplished through a specific price that is maintained for the customer project and work package.

Figure 1: Project planning in the Plan Customer Projects app

For billing, standard T&E with the contract material P005 is defined and both work packages are assigned to one billing item. As explained above, this new material would determine the new revenue recognition key SPTMSR.

Figure 2: Billing part of customer project in Plan Customer Projects app

The next step involves using the job application "Transfer Project Forecast to Finance" to populate the PRO03 plan category in the financial planning tables. If a forecast isn't maintained in the Review Customer Projects application, as illustrated in this example, the application job considers the ongoing plan.

Please note that the product (e.g., P005) can serve as a filter criterion in the job application. This method allows the transfer of forecast values solely for those WBS Elements (across various customer projects) that were configured for the use the revenue recognition key SPTMSR. This approach considerably reduces the runtime of the job application.

Figure 3: The application job Transfer Project Forecast to Finance

Figure 4: Plan category PRO03 in the Projects – Plan/Actual application

In this example scenario, the customer project is now up and running, and 10 hours have actually been booked on the first work package. Contrary to the standard T&E revenue recognition key, SPTM, these actual bookings lead to a recognized revenue of 954.55 EUR. When using SPTM, the recognized revenue would have been 1000 EUR.

𝑅𝑒𝑐𝑜𝑔𝑛𝑖𝑧𝑒𝑑 𝑅𝑒𝑣𝑒𝑛𝑢𝑒  = (𝐴𝑐𝑡𝑢𝑎𝑙 𝐶𝑜𝑠𝑡𝑠/𝑃𝑙𝑎𝑛𝑛𝑒𝑑 𝐶𝑜𝑠𝑡) ∗ 𝑃𝑙𝑎𝑛𝑛𝑒𝑑 𝑅𝑒𝑣𝑒𝑛𝑢𝑒

                                   = (650 EUR / 7,150 EUR) * 10,500 EUR

                                   = 954.55 EUR

Forecasted costs as well as forecasted revenue would be considered if maintained in the Review Customer Projects application.

Figure 5: Event-Based Revenue Recognition after posting 10h actuals

 

Additional considerations, especially for Finance

So far, planned revenues in revenue recognition have been calculated either based on T&E billing simulation (Time and Expenses, Usage Based Billing) or the billing plan (Fixed Price, Periodic Service). For the first time, the new method explained in this blog uses financial planning values from ACDOCP, including ETC/EAC values. This also leads to a different behaviour for repricing and the cap scenario, as outlined below.

The ETC/EAC values entered in the Review Customer Projects application are also considered when using SPTMSR This remains true even if you do not sustain forecasts; as a result, the application job is solely based on ongoing planning.

After running the application job "Transfer Project Forecast to Finance"

  1. Revenue recognition for already existing cost postings is revaluated based on the most current available EAC data in the revenue recognition run.
  2. The updated plan values for cost and revenue are displayed in the Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Projects application.

Although a cap can be defined on T&E contract types, its amount is ignored when the revenue recognition key SPTMSR is used. Additionally, write-offs will reduce the EAC revenues, but EAC cost will not be In contrast to the standard T&E revenue recognition, writing off cost items will not directly update the recognized revenue in EBRR.

In similar terms, a repricing run does not update unbilled revenue in EBRR for billing elements with SPTMSR. Instead, changes in sales rates must be reflected in updated PRO03 plan values. For this, the planned revenue in the work package needs to be recalculated before transferring the plan data to finance.

Please remember to always synchronize the project financial data with finance through the application job "Transfer Project Forecast to Finance" before running revenue recognition at the end of the period. This ensures up-to-date data in the financial planning version PRO03, even if no dedicated forecast is maintained.

One limitation to mention is that when cost postings already exist, the revenue recognition key can only be changed to "no revenue recognition key" and back.

 

Summary

T&E billing, in combination with PoC-based revenue recognition, allows for billing as per work performed (T&E), but realizes revenues based on Cost-based PoC. This provides an increased level of flexibility in financials. Additionally, manual efforts to adjust the accrued revenues can be avoided.
